config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I do not think society should automatically come before the rights of the individual, because history shows that “society’s interests” can be used to justify abuse, censorship, and unfair treatment. In my view, individual rights are the basic guardrails that keep power from becoming too heavy-handed, while society still benefits when people are protected and treated fairly. From my perspective, the best approach is to balance both, but never treat the individual as disposable for the sake of the group.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu diprioritaskan di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat betapa mudahnya alasan “demi masyarakat” dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau penyalahgunaan kekuasaan, jadi hak individu harus jadi batas yang kuat, bukan hal yang bisa dikorbankan sesuka hati. Tentu ada keadaan tertentu, seperti keselamatan publik atau keadaan darurat, di mana pembatasan sementara bisa dibenarkan, tetapi itu harus sempit, jelas, dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Kalau tidak, “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu mudah berubah menjadi alat untuk menekan orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “for the public good” can be used to silence dissent or excuse unfair treatment. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation, since public safety, disaster response, and basic social order do matter. But the burden should always be on society to justify any limit, and those limits must be narrow, temporary, and accountable. If we start by assuming the group always matters more, it becomes too easy to treat ordinary people as expendable.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu; dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, hak individu harus menjadi titik awal, bukan sesuatu yang otomatis kalah oleh “kepentingan masyarakat.” Saya paham argumen tentang keselamatan publik dan keadaan darurat, tetapi pengalaman politik di AS menunjukkan bahwa alasan demi kebaikan bersama sering dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau perluasan kekuasaan yang berlebihan. Kepentingan masyarakat penting, tetapi justru paling sehat jika dibatasi oleh hak yang jelas, karena tanpa batas itu kelompok rentan paling mudah dikorbankan. Jadi bagi saya, yang benar bukan memprioritaskan masyarakat secara umum, melainkan menyeimbangkannya dengan syarat bahwa pembatasan hak harus sempit, sementara, dan benar-benar bisa dipertanggungjawabkan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I reject the statement because in Indonesia "society first" is too often used to pressure people into silence, conformity, or unfair treatment. Our culture values harmony and gotong royong, but I do not think that means an individual should be sacrificed whenever the majority claims it is for the common good. Public safety and social order matter, yet any restriction on rights should be narrowly justified, temporary, and accountable, not treated as the default. If society is given priority too easily, the people with the least power are usually the first to lose their dignity.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak pernyataan itu karena, dari perspektif saya di Amerika Serikat, hak individu harus tetap menjadi dasar utama, bukan dikalahkan oleh klaim umum tentang kepentingan masyarakat. Pengalaman politik di AS menunjukkan bahwa alasan “demi kebaikan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan sensor, diskriminasi, atau perluasan kekuasaan yang berlebihan, jadi saya tidak percaya masyarakat boleh punya prioritas otomatis. Saya paham bahwa keselamatan publik dan keadaan darurat bisa menuntut pembatasan tertentu, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, sementara, dan bisa diuji secara ketat. Tanpa batas yang kuat, yang paling rentan justru paling mudah dikorbankan atas nama mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
